_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to identify where data and information come from and whether those sources are reliable.

_source.w: We are learning to identify where data and information come from and whether those sources are reliable.
y: Knowing where information comes from helps us decide how much to trust it.
Evaluating sources protects us from being misled by biased or inaccurate data.
This critical skill is essential in a world full of information competing for our attention.
eg: Checking whether a website about a product is run by the company that makes it.
Comparing data from a government report with data from a company's advertisement.
Discussing why some sources might present information in a biased way.
Investigating whether the data in a news article comes from a reliable study or just an opinion.
Evaluating the credibility of different sources when researching an economic question.
Creating a checklist for assessing whether a source of information is trustworthy.
